description: Join adjacent blocks within the given range. Joined blocks act as one block with a common centroid and volume calculated for all blocks joined together. A joined block group consists of one “leader” block and one or more “follower” blocks. Use the block list information command to identify leader and follower blocks in a block group. Note: only visible blocks are joined (see block hide ). This command is the same as block join except that contacts are used to identify the range.
